channel.title: Posts on fhlipZero.io
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on fhlipZero.io

item.title: Playing_with_payloads
item.description: 00_Intro I keep playing with different payloads as they come across twitter, they are usually good for a day or 2 max then Defender / everyone else starts catching it, figure I spent time playing with each of them so best to document it somewhere. Need to play with other general shellcode loaders and examples i have from the mr-un1kod3r course: https://github.com/Mr-Un1k0d3r/Shellcoding urbanbishop ebowla scarecrow pezor alaris urbanbishop https://github.com/slyd0g/UrbanBishopLocal Super simple - git clone, make raw payload in cobalt strike, convert it, add it to the VS project and replace the XOR key, build for x64.

item.title: How_M1_Macs_Affect_Internal_PenTests
item.description: Title options How Apples M1 Silicon has affected Internal Penetration Testing clickbait? Does deploying M1 Macs mean greater security for your business and trouble for your next red team engagement? Apples release of their M1 SoC (system-on-chip) in late 2020 has proven to be a processing and efficiency powerhouse, bringing technology like Rosetta 2, allowing the lines to blur between the strong iPad Pro and desktop mac. As with any new technology, the intrigue quickly shifts to concern as to how long it will be till we see one of these in the wild.

item.title: Dante
item.description: Dante HTB Pro Lab Review ![Pasted image 20210215215539.png tldr pivots c2_usage TLDR: Dante is an awesome lab (im avoid the use of the word beginner here) that combines pivoting, customer exploitation, and simple enumeration challenges into one fun environment. I say fun after having left and returned to this lab 3 times over the last months since its release. The important thing to remember is keeping it simple, common exploits and credentials re-use are rampant in Dante so be sure to harvest creds from each compromised host and grep each one for the “DANTE{” flag in case you leave one behind.

item.title: Weblogic Replication Ftw Kinda
Tripping Over Xss
item.description: Quite literally, tripping over XSS While working an engagement, I was manually soft stepping through https://github.com/swisskyrepo/PayloadsAllTheThings/tree/master/XSS%20Injection and noting things as I went when all of the sudden… This was reported to Laurent / Joplin and a fix implented in the following major version, not the most exciting finding but still a first CVE for me. https://www.exploit-db.com/exploits/49024 # Exploit Title: Joplin 1.2.6 Cross Site Scripting # Date: 2020-09-21 # Exploit Author: Philip Holbrook (@fhlipZero) # Vendor Homepage: https://joplinapp.
